message:
feat(process): case-insensitive environment variable keys on Windows via CompareStringOrdinal

Adds EnvKey wrapper that provides platform-correct environment variable
key comparison: byte-exact on Unix/WASM, case-insensitive on Windows
using the Win32 CompareStringOrdinal API (which matches the kernel's
actual environment block semantics).

The Unix/WASM EnvKey is a simple newtype around OsString with derived
Eq/Ord. The Windows EnvKey stores both the OsString and a pre-computed
UTF-16 representation, implementing Ord via CompareStringOrdinal with
LOCALE_INVARIANT and bIgnoreCase=TRUE. PartialEq and Eq are derived
from the Ord implementation for consistency.

This fixes a bug where setting PATH and Path as separate environment
variables on Windows would create duplicate entries in the child process
environment block, with the last one silently winning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
